tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated creatinine, marked proteinuria, and abnormal urine findings (cloudy, brown, low specific gravity, bilirubinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria), suggesting significant renal impairment, possibly nephrotic syndrome or acute/chronic kidney disease. HbA1c is low, ruling out chronic hyperglycemia. No evidence of urinary tract infection. Recommend urgent nephrology evaluation and further assessment for underlying renal pathology.

display.value: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
